headline: Delta Air Lines (DAL) Is Up 7.9% After Raising Q1 Revenue Outlook Amid Higher Fuel Costs
summary: Earlier this month, Delta Air Lines raised its first-quarter revenue outlook at the JPMorgan Industrials Conference, citing accelerating travel demand despite higher jet fuel costs linked to Middle East tensions. Management highlighted that eight of the ten strongest sales days in Delta’s history occurred in the recent quarter, underscoring momentum in both leisure and business bookings. Next, we’ll explore how Delta’s upgraded revenue outlook amid elevated fuel costs could influence the...

headline: Trump threatens to deploy ICE agents to airports if DHS shutdown doesn't end, while Elon Musk offers to cover TSA agents' pay
summary: President Trump claimed ICE agents handling airport security would arrest immigrants who are in the U.S. illegally.

headline: Elon Musk Offers To Cover TSA Salaries Amid Funding Crisis: 'Negatively Affecting Lives of So Many Americans'
summary: Elon Musk has said that he wants to cover Transportation Security Administration paychecks while a federal funding lapse leaves airport screeners working without salaries.

headline: Travel Demand Soars Despite Fuel Costs—Are Airline Stocks a Buy?
summary: Airlines boost outlooks despite higher fuel costs as travel demand surges. Some stocks look attractive, but analysts remain mixed on which carriers to buy.

headline: 3 Market Segments I'm Targeting When Iran War Ends
summary: IWM gives broad small-cap exposure hit by oil-price shocks. XLY stands to benefit when oil prices drop, as will JETS. Read here for more analysis.

headline: How does the current decline in airline stocks compare to historical periods?
summary: Investing.com -- Major U.S. airlines are approaching a critical inflection point as the industry grapples with a sudden spike in energy costs that threatens to upend full-year profitability. According to a research note from UBS, several carriers are expected to issue mid-quarter updates this week, with analysts predicting a widespread suspension of fiscal year 2026 guidance.

headline: Opinion: As Trump eyes Cuba, my trips there a decade ago remind me how different things were
summary: Trump has threatened a 'friendly takeover' of Cuba after cutting it off from Venezuela's oil. CNBC's Justin Solomon reflects how different things used to be.

headline: United Airlines Slashes Flights By 5% As Iran War Spikes Fuel Costs— CEO Scott Kirby Warns Of $175 Oil Scenario
summary: United Airlines is cutting 5% of its flights and bracing for a prolonged fuel cost surge driven by the Iran conflict, even as strong travel demand allows the airline to raise fares and cushion the financial impact.
